title: Full Stack Engineer
description: Safiri AI is redefining how goods move around the world by building a unified digital logistics platform that connects road, sea, and rail into one seamless experience. The company focuses on making cross-border shipping simple, transparent, and reliable, especially for smaller and growing businesses. By creating shared digital infrastructure, Safiri AI enables organizations of all sizes to operate globally with the confidence and capability of large enterprises. Headquartered in Sydney, Australia, the company shapes long-term strategy and partnerships there, while its engineering team in India drives world-class product development and scaling. Safiri AI’s vision is to become the operating system for global logistics so that shipping worldwide feels as straightforward as shipping locally. We are looking for Fullstack Engineers with 3–5 years of experience who can own the full delivery cycle, from understanding client requirements to building, deploying, and supporting production-ready tools. This role is ideal for someone who can work independently across the full stack, communicate directly with stakeholders, and turn evolving business needs into reliable software. Due to the high volume of applications we receive, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Applications must be submitted through our careers page. Roles and Responsbilities: Gather, clarify, and document requirements from clients, product owners, and stakeholders. Translate business needs into technical specifications, user stories, and implementation plans. Participate in discovery sessions, client calls, demos, and feedback loops before and after implementation. Design, build, and maintain backend services and REST APIs using NestJS, Node.js, and TypeScript. Develop responsive, performant frontend applications and admin tools using React, TypeScript, and Tailwind. Build internal tools, dashboards, automation utilities, and workflow systems end-to-end. Design and manage PostgreSQL database schemas, migrations, indexes, and queries using Prisma or similar ORMs. Own feature delivery from scoping and estimation through development, testing, deployment, and handoff. Supervise student interns in AI and software engineering by setting goals, assigning tasks, reviewing progress, and defining weekly milestones. Mentor junior engineers through code reviews, pair programming, walkthroughs, and knowledge-sharing sessions. Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests using tools such as Jest, Vitest, React Testing Library, Playwright, or Cypress. Support UAT, client acceptance, onboarding, training, and post-launch fixes. Integrate third-party APIs, webhooks, and external services. Troubleshoot integration issues, production bugs, and deployment problems. Use AI coding and agent tools daily to support prototyping, development, debugging, refactoring, test generation, and documentation. Integrate AI and LLM capabilities into product features where applicable. Review, test, and validate all AI-generated code before shipping. Create and maintain technical documentation, including API docs, runbooks, release notes, and client-facing technical notes. Monitor production usage, respond to incidents, and improve systems based on client feedback and operational data. Participate in sprint planning, code reviews, technical discussions, and delivery planning. Collaborate with designers to create user experiences that match real operational workflows. Communicate progress, risks, blockers, and trade-offs clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Optimize application and API performance and identify scalability bottlenecks early. Attend relevant logistics, trade, and enterprise software seminars or conferences. Complete assigned coursework on global logistics and clearing and forwarding to build domain understanding. What are we looking for: 3–5 years of professional experience delivering fullstack software in a product or client-facing environment. Proven ability to understand vague or evolving requirements and turn them into tested, shippable features. Strong experience with TypeScript, Node.js, NestJS, Express, or similar backend frameworks. Strong experience with React 18+ and modern frontend development. Experience designing REST APIs, validation flows, authentication, JWT, and role-based access control. Strong PostgreSQL experience, including schema design, migrations, indexing, and query optimization. ORM experience with Prisma or equivalent tools. Experience with state management and modern data-fetching patterns such as TanStack Query or similar. Experience with Tailwind or similar CSS frameworks. Ability to build responsive, accessible, and user-friendly interfaces. Strong Git workflow experience, including pull requests, code reviews, and branching practices. Familiarity with Docker and basic cloud or deployment environments. Experience with CI/CD pipelines. Testing experience with Jest, Vitest, React Testing Library, Playwright, Cypress, or similar tools. Daily experience using AI-powered development tools such as Cursor, Google Antigravity, Claude Code, Claude API, GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, OpenAI API, or similar agentic coding tools. Experience integrating LLM APIs into applications. Practical prompt engineering experience for code generation, debugging, documentation, or application features. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Comfortable leading short demos, walkthroughs, and technical discussions with clients or internal stakeholders. Self-directed execution style with the ability to scope work, estimate effort, and deliver without constant hand-holding. Willingness to travel internationally to Australia, India, or Tanzania when required. Willingness to attend seminars and complete structured coursework on global logistics and clearing and forwarding. Flexibility for weekend client meetings or critical production support, with additional compensation for approved weekend work. Availability to work in USA Central Time or an Australia-aligned timezone, with 4–6 hours of overlap with US CT or Australian AEST/AEDT. Benefits: Competitive salary based on location and experience. Remote-friendly work aligned to US Central Time or Australian business hours. Additional compensation for approved weekend client meetings or critical support. Support for required travel to Australia, India, or Tanzania. Professional development through logistics and clearing and forwarding coursework. Access to industry seminars, webinars, and relevant conferences. Direct ownership of client-facing tools and production features. Opportunity to supervise interns and mentor junior engineers. Modern technology stack with an AI-first development culture. Work on practical enterprise software for logistics, trade, and supply chain operations. Equal opportunity employer. We welcome applicants from diverse backgrounds and geographies.

title: Full Stack Developer
description: As a Developer at GoMeddo, you will become part of our talented Development team and play a key role in developing innovative software. You think of practical solutions to solve complex, abstract problems and translate these into reliable and maintainable software. Part of your role is to use your creativity and skills to make a real contribution to the team. You will work on our GoMeddo and GoTallo products. GoMeddo helps our customers with complex scheduling questions, we help our education customers schedule classes, teachers and equipment, or help car manufacturers schedule test drives for their cars. GoTallo is our messaging platform that enables seamless communication between businesses and their customers. Your background: Experience: At least 2 years of professional software development experience. Tech Stack: Strong knowledge of Python, JavaScript, and/or React. Cloud: Hands-on experience with AWS or similar cloud platforms. Nice-to-haves: Experience with Salesforce (Apex, LWC) or DevOps practices is a major plus. Location & Language: Currently living in the Netherlands, with fluent written and spoken English skills. What we offer: Compensation: A competitive salary based on your knowledge and experience. Growth: A generous Training & Education Budget, because we care about your professional development. Flexibility: Flexible working hours, whether you start at 7 a.m. or 10 a.m., we focus on the impact you deliver. Hybrid Work: Freedom to choose where you work (from home or our great office in Laren), though we love catching up face-to-face regularly. Culture: A high-trust, ego-free ecosystem where we assume positive intent. We operate as one united team, celebrate wins together, and maintain a safe space for experimentation where you can take risks Who are we? At GoMeddo we think technology should enable you to make life easier and improve the quality of experiences on a daily basis. We think flexibility and adaptability should be just as important as a strong digital backbone to run your business on and deliver user experiences with. That’s why we have combined these topics in 2014 and since then brought our Salesforce-based planning, scheduling and booking solution. It’s our mission to help organisations maximise their efficiency, user experience and grow their business with our native Salesforce planning, scheduling and booking application. Let’s free up resources, assets, spaces, people and customers and maximise their impact. We have got the app and the team who deliver on this promise in 34 countries across 6 continents, with millions of recurring reservations. Do you recognise yourself in the skills described and are you excited about possibly joining GoMeddo? Then apply by sending your CV to jobs@gomeddo.com.

title: Full Stack / Backend Software Engineer
description: About Rendy Rendy is a peer-to-peer marketplace that connects people who own high-quality equipment (cameras, gear, etc.) with people who need to rent it. We are building the engine that powers the sharing economy, allowing everyday individuals to monetize their unused gear safely and seamlessly. As an early engineering hire, you won't just be fixing bugs; you will be architecting the core platform, building complex payment routing systems, and shaping the foundation of our two-sided marketplace. The Role We are looking for a strong Software Developer with deep backend expertise to build out Rendy's core infrastructure. You will be responsible for building a robust system that handles user matching, booking states, escrow logic, and complex transaction ledgers. We are looking for someone who is a self-starter and highly self-motivated . You must be eager to learn , adaptable, and open-minded when it comes to adopting new technologies or pivoting architectures as our startup grows. Key Responsibilities System Architecture: Design, build, and document robust RESTful and tRPC APIs in [Node.js / TypeScript] to serve our web and mobile frontends. Microservices Implementation: Architect and maintain scalable backend services, demonstrating a strong understanding of microservices architecture to ensure our platform can grow modularly. Database Design: Architect and optimize our PostgreSQL database to handle complex marketplace logic, including booking states, transaction ledgers, inventory availability, and user profiles. Frontend Collaboration: Work closely with the frontend team to establish clear API contracts and utilize mocking strategies to ensure parallel development. Security & Compliance: Ensure user data and transaction flows are highly secure, implementing proper authentication, authorization, and data validation. Testing: Write comprehensive unit and integration tests to ensure our booking engine is bulletproof. What you bring to the table Experience: 3+ years of professional experience in backend or full-stack software development. Core Stack: Strong proficiency in Node.js (or similar backend environments like Go/Python) and PostgreSQL . You know how to write efficient queries and design normalized database schemas. Microservices: A strong understanding of microservices architecture, including inter-service communication, independent deployments, and service boundary design. Marketplace Experience: Prior experience building two-sided marketplaces, e-commerce platforms, or complex booking systems is highly preferred. API Design: You know how to design clean, predictable APIs. Experience with tRPC and end-to-end type safety is a major plus. Mindset: You are an open-minded self-starter who thrives in an autonomous environment and is always eager to learn new skills to solve complex problems. Nice to haves Familiarity with frontend frameworks (React, Vue, or Next.js) to help unblock the UI team. Experience setting up local development environments and CI/CD pipelines. Knowledge of cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP) and containerization (Docker). What we offer Competitive salary range Flexible working hours and remote-friendly culture The opportunity to build a platform from the ground up and have a massive impact on the product direction.

title: Full Stack Engineer
description: About Code for Good At Code for Good, we connect data, people and AI to help organisations work smarter, faster and more sustainably. We don't do experiments, we deliver measurable return. Technology is the means, not the goal. Our team designs and builds the Code for Good Platform, a system that turns raw data into real-world impact across six areas of intelligence: Vision, Document, Knowledge, Internal, External and Numbers. With Vision we turn camera feeds into usable data for inspection, sorting and quality control. Document structures information from paperwork and unstructured files, and Knowledge makes an organisation's collective know-how searchable and usable. Where a standard solution isn't enough, we run innovation tracks: custom development built together with the client and aimed at direct impact. We work across manufacturing, construction and infra, transport and logistics, food and agri, circular resources, and government and defence. The future of work, as we see it, runs on augmented intelligence: people and technology reinforcing each other rather than one replacing the other. The Role We're looking for a Full-Stack Developer who designs and builds complex systems end-to-end. You'll join our Platform team and own real pieces of software: APIs, integrations, frontends, and the infrastructure that runs them. We build our own platform and extend it to serve our clients' needs, and those needs are broad. One capability might be a document extraction flow, the next a pipeline that ingests camera data and surfaces classifications in an operator dashboard. The needs are wide-ranging, so the platform has to be too. This is a hands-on role across the stack. You're comfortable shipping a frontend feature one day and tuning a deployment pipeline the next, and you think about how systems run in production, not just how they're built. Our stack is TypeScript and Next.js on the application layer, Go for real-time and streaming services, and Python where it fits for data processing and model integration. We run on Azure, and we also manage physical machines and cameras on client sites, so the work runs from cloud down to edge hardware. Devops and infra are part of the job, not an afterthought: CI/CD, deployments, and keeping things reliable in production. We give our engineers (AI) tooling, and expect you to use it well. We care about what you ship and how it holds up, not how much you type. What you will do Design and build full-stack applications, from data model to UI Make architectural decisions across services, data, and integrations Integrate with external systems and hardware: ERPs, cameras, sensors, ... Work with AI and vision models in production What you need to succeed A few years building software in production Strong with TypeScript/JavaScript and Next.js or React Strong in at least one backend language: C++, Rust, Go, or similar Solid backend fundamentals: data modeling, API design, async flows Comfortable owning a feature end-to-end Real depth in at least one of: Platforms and SaaS: multi/single-tenancy, data models, clean APIs, the layer customers live in Real-time and streaming: event-driven systems and processing camera or sensor feeds in production Nice to have Python, Azure, LLM APIs Comfort with, or interest in, physical machines and edge hardware What We Offer Real ownership and freedom to shape our platform and technology An open culture where your ideas turn into action quickly Salary €4.000 - €6.500+ depending on experience and impact. Growth opportunities as we build and scale together A friendly work environment in the center of Eindhoven Additional Information Office-first culture, based in the centre of Eindhoven Small team with direct impact on product and technology decisions Flat hierarchy with short lines of communication We are not our first startup and have experience building and scaling technology businesses Full-time role (40 hours per week) Pension plan included MacBook Pro provided Learning and development budget available, discussed together based on your goals and ambitions Please note This is an office-first position in Eindhoven Applicants must already live in the Netherlands within commuting distance of the office We cannot offer visa sponsorship at this time

Proficiency with Git and modern CI/CD practices. A genuine interest in product development. You want to work with the team from our Stockholm office, where we collaborate in person most days. Humble, collaborative, and open to working across the full stack. Should have Strong skills in TypeScript. Professional experience with SQL, preferably PostgreSQL. Familiarity with ORMs and modern frontend frameworks. Interest in Hygglo’s business and mission. Curiosity about how AI and automation can improve products, workflows, and user experiences. Nice to have Understanding of performance optimization in React Native. Experience with Expo and mobile app distribution via App Store and Google Play. Experience with payment systems, such as Stripe, and/or fraud detection. Deep backend or database expertise. Experience developing native modules for iOS or Android. Why Join Hygglo? Grow and learn At Hygglo, engineers and designers work across the stack and disciplines, while also having the space to deepen expertise in their own area. Startup spirit, real scale Be part of a small, motivated team within a growing international company, where your ideas are heard and your work reaches users across Europe. A supportive culture We celebrate humility, learning, and helping each other succeed. We know innovation comes from collaboration, not ego. Competitive salary and benefits We want you to thrive both inside and outside of work.

title: Software Engineer - Full Stack
description: About The Role The role is responsible for building and scaling high-performance web applications, spanning from responsive, user-facing frontend interfaces to robust, low-latency backend services. The team designs and maintains core product features that serve millions of active users daily, emphasizing modular architecture, system reliability, and seamless user experiences. This position collaborates closely with product managers, product designers, and infrastructure engineers to translate complex requirements into clean, scalable code. The engineer will play a critical role in shaping the technical direction of the web platform, optimizing application performance, and establishing modern software engineering practices across the stack. Key Responsibilities Develop, test, and deploy end-to-end features using React, TypeScript, Node.js, and Python across a distributed microservices architecture Design and optimize relational and non-relational database schemas using PostgreSQL, Redis, and MongoDB for high-throughput applications Build and maintain secure, well-documented RESTful and GraphQL APIs to support frontend applications and third-party integrations Optimize application performance and load times on the frontend, utilizing code-splitting, caching strategies, and efficient state management Write comprehensive unit, integration, and end-to-end tests using Jest, Cypress, and React Testing Library to ensure high code quality and prevent regressions Participate in design reviews, peer code reviews, and contribute to CI/CD pipeline automation using Docker, GitHub Actions, and AWS What We Are Looking For 3–6 years of professional software engineering experience building and maintaining production-grade web applications at scale Deep technical proficiency in JavaScript/TypeScript, modern React, and backend runtimes such as Node.js, Go, or Python Strong experience with database design, query optimization, and data modeling in SQL (PostgreSQL, MySQL) and NoSQL environments Solid understanding of web fundamentals, including browser rendering pipelines, security best practices (CORS, CSRF, JWT), and HTTP protocols Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience Bonus: Experience with AWS cloud services (ECS, Lambda, RDS), GraphQL federation, or modern CSS frameworks like Tailwind
